2014-05-01.title: Fans Lose Soccer Team, Then Build Their Own

2014-05-01.abstract: The meager expectations of Hamdeen Sabahi, who is opposing the former general Abdul-Fattah el-Sisi in the race for Egypt’s presidency, illustrate the breakdown of Egypt’s political life since the presidential election two years ago.

2014-05-01.abstract: A 2012 email from Benjamin J. Rhodes, the deputy national security adviser, includes goals for Susan E. Rice’s appearances on TV talk shows and advice on how to discuss the protests that were raging in Libya.

2014-05-02.abstract: The collapse of the illegal gold mine occurred late Wednesday night in western Colombia, and rescuers were searching for other miners believed to be missing.

2014-05-25.title: Chinese Flybys Alarm Japan as Tensions Escalate
2014-05-25.abstract: The Japanese Defense Ministry said Chinese jet fighters had flown dangerously close to its reconnaissance planes in two separate incidents.

2014-05-25.title: Pope, in Mideast, Invites Leaders to Meet on Peace
2014-05-25.abstract: Wading into the heart of Middle East tensions, Pope Francis also endorsed “the State of Palestine” in symbolic visits to the West Bank and Jerusalem.

2014-05-25.title: Suspects in China Market Attack Are Identified
2014-05-25.abstract: Four of the men were killed when the two vehicles they used to mount the attack exploded in Urumqi, the capital of the western region of Xinjiang.

2014-05-26.title: Press Battle in Pakistan Feeds Into Larger Conflict: Government vs. Military
2014-05-26.abstract: A spat over the shooting of a talk show host, followed by the government’s reversal of a ban on the station, has exposed tensions between Prime Minister Nawaz Sharif and top officials.

2014-05-26.title: Lebanon Arrests Radical Cleric Who Backed Militants
2014-05-26.abstract: Omar Bakri Muhammad, a Syrian-born cleric who preached for years in London, had expressed support for the extremist group Islamic State of Iraq and Syria.

2014-05-26.title: Once Bustling, Syria’s Fractured Railroad Is a Testament to Shattered Ambitions
2014-05-26.abstract: The shutdown of the Hejaz railway station in Damascus reflects the lost links of business and society that once connected the region.

2014-05-26.title: In East, Separatists and Fear Stop Voters
2014-05-26.abstract: Just 2 percent of registered voters in Donetsk are said to cast ballot, and Western observers said the election would be legal, even without turnout from the eastern provinces.

2014-05-26.title: Global Digital News Brands See Growth Opportunity in India
2014-05-26.abstract: BuzzFeed, Huffington Post and Atlantic Media are among the companies with plans to build major online news operations in India, both on their own and with local partners.

2014-05-27.title: To Prepare for the World Cup, a Host City Could Use Extra Time
2014-05-27.abstract: When the World Cup begins next month, many of the $1.4 billion-worth of projects in Cuiabá, one of the 12 Brazilian host cities, will be far from completion.

2014-05-27.title: Nigerian Military Says It Knows Location of Abducted Girls
2014-05-27.abstract: The military commander said he would not disclose further details out of concern for the girls’ safety.

2014-05-27.title: U.S. Training Elite Antiterror Troops in Four African Nations
2014-05-27.abstract: United States Special Operations troops are forming elite units in Libya, Niger, Mauritania and Mali in the war against Al Qaeda’s affiliates in Africa.

2014-05-27.title: Spain: Village Votes for New Name
2014-05-27.abstract: Residents of Castrillo Matajudíos, which loosely translates as Little Fort of Jew Killers, have voted to change the name of their village.

2014-05-27.title: The Appeal of the Fringe
2014-05-27.abstract: In the elections for the European Parliament over the weekend, many traditional, centrist political parties lost ground to smaller or upstart parties from both sides of the spectrum.

2014-05-28.title: Dissenters in Egypt Mock Election Turmoil
2014-05-28.abstract: With the expected coronation of Abdel Fattah el-Sisi falling flat, schadenfreude was the dominant emotion of Egyptian activists and bloggers.

2014-05-28.title: Rebelling Against Abuse, Afghan Women See Signs of Change
2014-05-28.abstract: The case of an Afghan woman who set her husband on fire when he refused to act after she had been raped has highlighted the promise and limits of new protections for abused women.

2014-05-28.title: Tiananmen Square Anniversary Prompts Campaign of Silence
2014-05-28.abstract: Censors and security forces have waged an aggressive “stability maintenance” crusade as the June 4th anniversary approaches, and the police have warned Western journalists to stay away from the square.

2014-05-28.title: For Middle East, Region of Religious Conflict, Pope Suggests a Respite in Prayer
2014-05-28.abstract: Betting on breakthroughs, or even positive developments, is always perilous in the Middle East, but Francis exhibited a deft touch during his visit.

2014-05-28.title: Nigeria Denies Formal Talks on Captive Schoolgirls
2014-05-28.abstract: A government spokesman did not rule out some type of discussion with people in contact with Boko Haram to try to win the freedom of hundreds of kidnapped schoolgirls.

2014-05-29.title: Crimean Vineyards of Last Czar Withstand Time and Tumult
2014-05-29.abstract: Founded by Czar Nicholas II in 1894, the Massandra vineyards carry a time capsule of Russian and world history in their expansive cellars.

2014-05-29.title: Israel Suspends Soldier in West Bank Shooting Investigation
2014-05-29.abstract: An Israeli soldier from a communications unit charged with documenting the work of combat troops was suspended for firing his rifle during a protest.

2014-05-29.title: Suicide Bomber in Syria Was U.S. Citizen, Officials Say
2014-05-29.abstract: A man who carried out a suicide bombing Sunday was believed to be the first American involved in such an attack, U.S. officials said.

2014-05-29.title: Terror’s Front: Local Groups, Eyes on West
2014-05-29.abstract: Decentralized Qaeda affiliates and extremists now pose “the most direct threat” to the United States and American interests abroad, the president said Wednesday.

2014-05-29.title: Leader of French Far Right Hopes to Fix Europe From the Inside
2014-05-29.abstract: Marine Le Pen, the leader of France’s National Front, outlined ambitions to reshape Europe’s direction during her visit to Brussels.

2014-05-30.title: An Island Marred by a Ferry Disaster and Sustained by Dogs
2014-05-30.abstract: Before a deadly ferry sinking in April, the South Korean island of Jindo was known largely for one thing: its dogs, famous for their loyalty and homing instinct.

2014-05-30.title: With Main Parties in Disarray, France’s Far Right Benefits
2014-05-30.abstract: In her recent election campaign, Marine Le Pen portrayed mainstream politicians as corrupt. As if on cue, the main conservative party acknowledged using false invoices in 2012.

2014-05-30.title: Diego Cordovez, U.N. Envoy and Conflict Mediator, Dies at 78
2014-05-30.abstract: Mr. Cordovez, an Ecuadorean, negotiated the withdrawal of Soviet troops from Afghanistan in the 1980s.

2014-05-30.title: N.S.A. Releases Email That It Says Undercuts Snowden’s Whistle-Blower Claim
2014-05-30.abstract: The message made no reference to government collection of data of the former contractor’s privacy concerns. Instead, he was seeking clarification of the laws governing the agency.

2014-05-30.title: At Core of Church-State Clash, Unease Over Christianity’s Rising Influence in China
2014-05-30.abstract: As Christianity continues its rapid rise in China, some believers feels discriminated against by public policies and say the government favors traditional Chinese religions.

2014-05-31.title: Conviction in Terrorism Case in Canada
2014-05-31.abstract: A jury on Friday convicted a former security guard from Toronto of attempting to join a foreign terrorist organization, a first for Canada.

2014-05-31.title: Bombing Thwarted, Israeli Military Says
2014-05-31.abstract: Israeli troops on Friday detained a Palestinian man who was wearing a belt packed with explosives at a West Bank intersection that has been the site of previous attacks, Israeli military officials said.

2014-05-31.title: In Spanish Riots, Anguish of Those Recovery Forgot
2014-05-31.abstract: Four nights of demonstrations in Spain’s tourism capital have highlighted the country’s persistent social tensions and belied signs of relief from years of austerity.

2014-05-31.title: U.S. Sway in Asia Is Imperiled as China Challenges Alliances
2014-05-31.abstract: As Defense Secretary Chuck Hagel visited Singapore, the Obama administration’s Asia policy appeared to be turning into a street fight, with the United States playing referee.

2014-05-31.title: Iran Hackers Dangle a Familiar Name to Fish for Data
2014-05-31.abstract: Hackers seeking information about Washington’s elite posed as John R. Bolton, a former American ambassador to the United Nations, to friend online users through social media.
